title: FI Skill Trainer
subtitle: Content from possible_skills.json in the overall evaluation layout
intro: Yes. If a trainee has mastered LF02 properly, a future employer can already assign many practical hardware/workstation/system-adjacent tasks.
I list them deliberately broad: from simple activities to tasks that require more thinking, justification, and responsibility.

subsections.title: Understand hardware and components
items: Name PC components and explain their tasks
Explain differences between CPU, RAM, SSD, HDD, motherboard, power supply, graphics unit
Identify components of an existing PC
Read datasheets roughly and extract relevant information
Explain pros and cons of different storage types
Recognize typical interfaces and name them correctly
Assign peripheral devices sensibly to a system

subsections.title: Plan and select workstations
items: Roughly assess hardware for developer workstations
Roughly assess hardware for media workstations
Derive a simple hardware recommendation from requirements
Translate customer requirements into technical requirements

subsections.title: Check compatibility
items: Check whether components basically fit together
Check whether a monitor connector fits
Check whether enough ports are available
Check whether storage solutions fit the intended use
Check whether a device is suitable for a workstation
Recognize when a system is not expansion-friendly
Recognize when an offer is technically inconsistent
Recognize when ports or capacities are missing
Recognize when a system misses the need

subsections.title: Set up and connect devices
items: Connect keyboard, mouse, printer, and other peripherals
Set up multiple identical workstations following a template

subsections.title: Workstation provisioning
items: Unbox and set up new PCs
Set up workstations ready for use
Connect monitors and input devices correctly

subsections.title: Troubleshooting and support
items: Perform first checks for a black screen
Narrow down initial causes for a slow PC
Perform basic checks for an unrecognized USB device
Recognize obvious bottlenecks in performance issues
Check errors systematically instead of hectically
Distinguish display problem vs network problem
Distinguish storage problem vs performance problem
Hand off to second-level support with usable pre-analysis

subsections.title: Maintenance and simple optimization
items: Recognize unnecessary files as a cause of low space
Give hints about SSD/HDD being too small
Give hints about too little RAM
Give hints about unsuitable legacy hardware
Justify standard recommendations for SSD instead of HDD
Recognize patterns in recurring performance problems

subsections.title: User and customer communication
items: Explain in simple language what CPU, RAM, and SSD mean
Explain differences between hardware components to colleagues
Ask follow-up questions about intended use
Translate technical facts into non-technical language
Justify why a cheap computer is not automatically economical
Justify why need must come before price
Briefly present pros and cons of a solution

subsections.title: Documentation
items: Document results from tests and comparisons

subsections.title: Calculation and estimation
items: Roughly estimate how much storage is needed
Recognize unrealistic statements from customers or offers

subsections.title: Quality and evaluation
items: Weigh criteria such as price, performance, storage, ports, and maintainability
Evaluate training room hardware differently than individual workstations

subsections.title: Typical mini tasks in a company
items: "Check why the monitor shows no image."
"Find three suitable office PCs for us."
"Check whether the connectors fit our monitors."
"Create a recommendation for 10 training room PCs."
"Check why there is no free storage left on the device."
"Document the equipment of the new PCs."
"Explain to the customer the difference between RAM and SSD."
"Do a short pre-analysis for the support ticket."
"Assess whether the cheap computer is actually sensible."
"Set up the new workstations in the meeting room."
"Write a short technical recommendation with justification."
